{
  "analysis": "Everything is complete and verified:

1. All 20 extracted rows match the true A1 with cosine similarity = 1.000000 (perfect match up to permutation and scaling)
2. The bidirectional matching confirms 20/20 unique rows matched in both directions
3. steal.py has no secret imports of A1 - it only uses the forward() function
4. Running `python3 /app/steal.py` reproduces the result deterministically
5. The extracted matrix is saved to /app/stolen_A1.npy with shape (20, 10)

The approach works by computing gradients via finite differences along random lines, detecting when the gradient changes (indicating a ReLU neuron boundary crossing), validating that the crossing is clean (single boundary), and collecting the gradient differences which are proportional to rows of A1 (scaled by the corresponding A2 coefficient).",
  "plan": "Task is complete. No further action needed.",
  "commands": [],
  "task_complete": true
}
